title: nymphoides aquatica
intro: Nymphoides aquatica, also known as water fringe or water wakeroot, is a perennial aquatic plant species native to North America. It is a rhizomatous herb with blue or purple flowers and is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, with its health benefits attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and alkaloids. Nymphoides aquatica is traditionally prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture for its herbal remedies, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine.
